description: Phase 2 evidence AI judgment eval — structural prescreen + per-case LLM stubs (includes adversarial claim-echo + numeric containment for capacity and %/Nx performance, plus missing-baseline disclosure cases)

cases.notes: Internal test memo vs PC201/PC200 capacity claim — methodology complete

context.ad_text: Cook for up to 8-10 people with PC201 multi-cooker.

evidence.title: CLM-012884 Internal capacity test memo
evidence.evidence_text: Product PC201/PC200 total stew weight measured 1.96–2.45 kg under standard fill test. Per FDA reference single-serving weight 245g/person. Calculation: 2450g ÷ 245g = 10 servings (conservative lower bound 8 servings). Method: full pot load, drained yield weighed on calibrated scale.

llm_stub_response.relevance_reasoning: FDA 245g methodology applied to PC201 measured yield.

cases.notes: SGS non-stick test for 40N1S — wrong SKU vs PC201 finding

context.ad_text: Cook for up to 8-10 people with PC201.

evidence.title: SGS SHES240100023371 Non-stick performance test
evidence.evidence_text: Report SHES240100023371: Rice cooker model 40N1S non-stick coating adhesion test.

context.ad_text: AF2024 uses 50% less oil.

evidence.title: Lab report for AF1000
evidence.evidence_text: Oil consumption comparison test for model AF1000.

context.ad_text: Strongest suction in its class — V15.
Reduces oil intake for healthier meals.

evidence.title: Internal kitchen test log
evidence.evidence_text: Internal memo: air fryer uses less oil than deep fryer in our kitchen demo.

llm_stub_response.sufficiency_reasoning: Would be sufficient if internal test were allowed.
llm_stub_response.extracted_key_facts: Less oil than deep fryer in kitchen demo.

context.ad_text: 1200W peak motor power.

evidence.title: Engineering bench test
evidence.evidence_text: Test method IEC 60335-2-14 clause 11. Method: wattmeter on BL500 unit SN0042, 3 samples avg 1195W peak. Raw data table attached.

llm_stub_response.relevance_reasoning: IEC test on BL500 with raw wattmeter data.
llm_stub_response.extracted_key_facts: 1195W peak avg of 3 samples per IEC 60335-2-14.

context.ad_text: SAFETY Mark registered V12 vacuum.

evidence.title: Internal compliance checklist
evidence.evidence_text: Internal team checked CPSR portal screenshot.

context.ad_text: 已通过CCC强制认证

evidence.title: CCC certificate
evidence.evidence_text: CCC certificate No. 202401070567890 for model XCQ001 vacuum cleaner, valid until 2029-01-01.

llm_stub_response.relevance_reasoning: Certificate lists XCQ001 and active validity.
llm_stub_response.sufficiency_reasoning: Official CCC certificate with registration number.
llm_stub_response.extracted_key_facts: CCC No. 202401070567890 for XCQ001 valid until 2029-01-01.

context.ad_text: No.1 rice cooker in Japan.

evidence.title: Sales dashboard export
evidence.evidence_text: Internal sales report Q1: RC100 top SKU in our JP channel.

llm_stub_response.relevance_reasoning: Internal sales data cited for ranking.
llm_stub_response.extracted_key_facts: Top SKU in JP channel Q1.

context.ad_text: More suction than Brand X under same test.

evidence.title: KOTRA consumer survey
evidence.evidence_text: Survey n=1200 households on brand preference only — no suction measurement.

llm_stub_response.relevance_reasoning: Survey measures preference not suction performance.
llm_stub_response.sufficiency_reasoning: No like-for-like suction measurement vs Brand X.

context.ad_text: Uses 40% less energy.

evidence.title: EU energy lab report
evidence.evidence_text: Energy test for AF88 under EU ERP protocol.

llm_stub_response.relevance_reasoning: SKU matches but test jurisdiction is DE not AU market.
llm_stub_response.sufficiency_reasoning: EU ERP protocol may not map to AU claim without local test.
llm_stub_response.extracted_key_facts: EU ERP energy test for AF88.

context.ad_text: SM700-Pro steams in 30 seconds.

evidence.title: Heat-up test SM700
evidence.evidence_text: Steam ready time 28–32 seconds for model SM700 (base model, not Pro variant).

llm_stub_response.relevance_reasoning: Base model SM700 tested; Pro variant claim may differ.
llm_stub_response.sufficiency_reasoning: Evidence does not cover SM700-Pro variant explicitly.

context.ad_text: Best performance.

cases.notes: Adversarial: evidence restates claim ('feed up to 8 people') with no method/data/date/institution. Production live result: relevance=strong, sufficiency=insufficient. Regression gate: sufficiency must stay insufficient — do not relax hollow-evidence checks when tuning numeric containment.

context.ad_text: Family-sized capacity - feed up to 8 people

evidence.title: Marketing confirmation memo (hollow)
evidence.evidence_text: This document confirms the following statement: 'The product has a family-sized capacity and can feed up to 8 people.' The above capacity has been confirmed and is accurate. This capacity meets the requirements for family-sized cooking appliances. Certified compliant for marketing use.

llm_stub_response.relevance_reasoning: Evidence text addresses the same capacity claim and matching '8 people' figure, but only by restating the claim.
llm_stub_response.sufficiency_reasoning: No test method, measured data, date, or issuing institution — circular claim-echo is not substantiation.
llm_stub_response.extracted_key_facts: Memo repeats 'feed up to 8 people' / 'confirmed accurate' with no measurements.

cases.notes: Claim 'up to 6 people' covered by CLM-style evidence supporting 8–10 under documented method. Must NOT be relevance=none from literal digit mismatch. Separate from adversarial-claim-echo — this case has real methodology.

context.ad_text: Family-sized capacity - feed up to 6 people with PC201 multi-cooker.

evidence.title: CLM-012884 Internal capacity test memo
evidence.evidence_text: Product PC201/PC200 total stew weight measured 1.96–2.45 kg under standard fill test. Per FDA reference single-serving weight 245g/person. Calculation: 2450g ÷ 245g = 10 servings (conservative lower bound 8 servings). Method: full pot load, drained yield weighed on calibrated scale.

llm_stub_response.relevance_reasoning: Evidence supports 8–10 servings for PC201; claimed upper bound of 6 is within / below that range (numeric containment).
llm_stub_response.sufficiency_reasoning: Documented weight ÷ 245g methodology yields 8–10; covers claim of up to 6 people.
llm_stub_response.extracted_key_facts: Measured 1.96–2.45 kg → 8–10 servings; claim asks for 6.

cases.notes: Claim 'up to 12 people' exceeds evidence upper bound 8–10. Must remain blocked (sufficiency insufficient; relevance none or partial — fixture locks none+insufficient). Do not weaken hollow-evidence fixtures to make this pass.

context.ad_text: Family-sized capacity - feed up to 12 people with PC201 multi-cooker.

evidence.title: CLM-012884 Internal capacity test memo
evidence.evidence_text: Product PC201/PC200 total stew weight measured 1.96–2.45 kg under standard fill test. Per FDA reference single-serving weight 245g/person. Calculation: 2450g ÷ 245g = 10 servings (conservative lower bound 8 servings). Method: full pot load, drained yield weighed on calibrated scale.

llm_stub_response.relevance_reasoning: Claimed upper bound of 12 exceeds evidence-supported range of 8–10 servings.
llm_stub_response.sufficiency_reasoning: Evidence upper bound (10) does not cover claim of 12 people.
llm_stub_response.extracted_key_facts: Evidence supports 8–10 servings; claim asks for 12.

cases.notes: Claim 'up to 70% faster' covered by evidence 'up to 2x faster'. Locked conversion: 2x faster = 100% faster (NOT 200%). Must NOT be relevance=none from digit/unit mismatch (70 vs 2x/100).

context.ad_text: 1200W Power to Cook Up to 70% faster* with HyperHeat technology. *Compared with slow cooking, simmering or braising.

evidence.title: CLM-013789 HyperHeat cook-time comparison
evidence.evidence_text: HyperHeat cook-time study vs conventional slow cooking / simmering / braising under controlled fill. Aggregate result: up to 2x faster (100% relative improvement). Selected recipes measured up to 3.82x and 6.74x. Method: timed cook cycles with calibrated timer; same ingredient load; baseline = slow cook / simmer / braise modes.

llm_stub_response.relevance_reasoning: Same cook-time faster metric vs slow cook/simmer/braise. Evidence up to 2x faster converts to 100% faster ((2-1)×100%), which covers the claimed 70% upper bound — not a digit mismatch.
llm_stub_response.sufficiency_reasoning: Documented timed comparison vs disclosed baseline; 2x=100% covers 70%.
llm_stub_response.extracted_key_facts: Up to 2x faster (=100% faster) vs slow cook/simmer/braise; some recipes 3.82x / 6.74x.

cases.notes: Same magnitude coverage as 2x=100%≥70%, but ad omits comparison baseline footnote. Must be relevance=partial + sufficiency=insufficient (missing disclosed baseline) — NOT relevance=none from 70 vs 100/2x digit mismatch.

context.ad_text: 1200W Power to Cook Up to 70% faster* with HyperHeat technology

evidence.title: CLM-013789 HyperHeat cook-time comparison
evidence.evidence_text: HyperHeat cook-time study vs conventional slow cooking / simmering / braising under controlled fill. Aggregate result: up to 2x faster (100% relative improvement). Selected recipes measured up to 3.82x and 6.74x. Method: timed cook cycles with calibrated timer; same ingredient load; baseline = slow cook / simmer / braise modes.

llm_stub_response.relevance_reasoning: Evidence covers cook-time faster magnitude (2x=100% ≥ 70%) on the same metric, but the ad text has '*' without disclosing the comparison baseline — not a numeric mismatch.
llm_stub_response.sufficiency_reasoning: Ad lacks a disclosed comparison baseline (vs slow cook / simmer / braise). Evidence numbers alone cannot clear the disclosure gap.
llm_stub_response.extracted_key_facts: Evidence up to 2x faster (=100% faster) vs slow cook; ad omits baseline footnote text.

cases.notes: No case product_sku; ad/claim do not name a model. Evidence self-declares 'PC20X | PC201/PC200' alias and supports 8–10 people under documented method. Must NOT be relevance=none / insufficient solely for literal PC20X vs missing ad model. Numeric containment (6 ≤ 8–10) + alias rule → strong+sufficient.

context.ad_text: Family-sized capacity - feed up to 6 people

evidence.title: CLM-012884 Internal capacity test memo
evidence.evidence_text: Product: PC20X | PC201/PC200 (alias map for marketing SKU vs test units). Total stew weight measured 1.96–2.45 kg under standard fill test. Per FDA reference single-serving weight 245g/person. Calculation: 2450g ÷ 245g = 10 servings (conservative lower bound 8 servings). Method: full pot load, drained yield weighed on calibrated scale.

llm_stub_response.relevance_reasoning: Case SKU not provided; ad/claim name no conflicting model. Evidence self-declares PC20X | PC201/PC200 alias, so literal PC20X is not a product mismatch. Capacity 8–10 covers claimed up to 6 (numeric containment).
llm_stub_response.sufficiency_reasoning: Documented weight ÷ 245g methodology yields 8–10 servings; covers claim of up to 6 people. Alias map is in the evidence text.
llm_stub_response.extracted_key_facts: PC20X | PC201/PC200 alias; measured 1.96–2.45 kg → 8–10 servings; claim asks for 6.

cases.notes: Locks the conversion rule itself: stub must treat 2x faster as 100% faster, never 200%. Claim 90% is still covered by 100%.

context.ad_text: Cook up to 90% faster* than conventional slow cooking with HyperHeat.

evidence.title: Internal timed cook study
evidence.evidence_text: Timed comparison vs conventional slow cooking: up to 2x faster under standard load. Method: calibrated timer; identical ingredients; baseline slow-cook mode.

llm_stub_response.relevance_reasoning: Convert 2x faster → (2-1)×100% = 100% faster; covers claimed 90%. Do not interpret 2x as 200% faster.
llm_stub_response.sufficiency_reasoning: Documented method vs disclosed conventional slow-cooking baseline; 100% covers 90%.
llm_stub_response.extracted_key_facts: Up to 2x faster = 100% faster (not 200%) vs conventional slow cooking.
